method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/lpc (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + Dynatrace + AWS ALB), the Discover NYC Landmarks map (Esri ArcGIS Online at nyclpc.maps.arcgis.com), and the Portico permit portal at portico.lpc.nyc.gov/s/ (identified as Salesforce Experience Cloud via the /s/ community path, Lightning/Aura/force.com markup, renderCtx and LSKey-c$ cookies). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Landmarks Preservation Commission (LPC)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 15 assets with column schemas.

meta.platform: NYC.gov 'Livesite' informational site (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace) + Esri ArcGIS Online (Discover NYC Landmarks map) + Salesforce Experience Cloud (Portico permit portal)
meta.netnew: LandmarkPermitApplication — file a Certificate of Appropriateness / No Effect / Minor Work application (today Salesforce Portico only, no API)
meta.tagline: LPC's landmark record is wide open but scattered across three vendor silos — Socrata data, an ArcGIS map, and a Salesforce permit portal — with no owned API binding them and no way to file a permit through code.
meta.headline: The landmark record is fully public yet unbound — split across Open Data, an Esri map, and a Salesforce portal — and the one transaction that matters, filing a Certificate of Appropriateness, is locked inside Portico.

scorecard_notes.platform: Three vendor platforms (Livesite, Esri ArcGIS Online, Salesforce Experience Cloud), none an owned LPC API surface.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content API on the info site; the Discover map is backed by ArcGIS Online feature services, queryable but not documented or owned as an LPC API.
scorecard_notes.openData: 15 Socrata datasets — landmarks, districts, building database, permit history, complaints, violations, grants, archaeology — richly published.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core designation entities well modeled and consistently keyed on LP_NUMBER and BBL/BIN; building database carries architect/style/materials/dates.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: Filing a permit (Certificate of Appropriateness / No Effect) has no API — it lives only in the Salesforce Portico portal.
scorecard_notes.agent: No MCP or agent-native surface today; this assessment proposes the first.
scorecard_notes.ownership: LPC publishes open data but owns no API; read is spread across Socrata + Esri and the write transaction is a Salesforce community.

headline_findings: LPC is a three-silo domain: an informational NYC.gov 'Livesite' site, the Discover NYC Landmarks map on Esri ArcGIS Online (nyclpc.maps.arcgis.com), and the Portico permit portal on Salesforce Experience Cloud (portico.lpc.nyc.gov/s/) — none of them an owned LPC API.

The reference data is unusually OPEN: 15 NYC Open Data datasets covering individual/interior/scenic landmarks, the Individual Landmark and Historic District Building Database (37 columns of architect/style/material/date), historic districts, designation and archaeology reports, complaints, and violations.

Unlike NYCHA, even the permit record is published — 'LPC Permit Application Information' (dpm2-m9mq, 32 columns) is an open READ twin of issued Certificates of Appropriateness / No Effect. What is missing is the WRITE side.

Filing a new permit application (Certificate of Appropriateness / No Effect / Minor Work) has no API at all — it happens only inside the Salesforce Portico community or on paper. That is the net-new write surface.
Everything is keyed on LP_NUMBER and BBL/BIN, so the three silos can be bound into one owned, LP_NUMBER-keyed contract. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 15 LPC datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open LPC API — designation, building, permit-history, and enforcement data.

apis_observed.public: map UI; ArcGIS REST feature services
apis_observed.note: Discover NYC Landmarks map. Backed by ArcGIS Online hosted feature services (queryable ArcGIS REST), but not documented or owned by LPC as an API.

Portico — file a Certificate of Appropriateness / No Effect / Minor Work application and track its status. Salesforce Lightning/Aura community (/s/ path, force.com, renderCtx/LSKey-c$ cookies); no JSON/OpenAPI surface.
content only — about, designations, permit guide, hearings; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM, AWS ALB. No content API.

fruit.name: Individual, Interior & Scenic Landmarks
opendata_match: Designated and Calendared Buildings and Sites ncre-qhxs (34c)
fruit.notes: Core designation unit. Keyed on LP_NUMBER; carries site status, hearing/designation dates, LM_TYPE, and the BBL/BIN spine. Scenic landmarks fold in as an LM_TYPE.

fruit.name: Landmark & Historic District Building Database
opendata_match: Individual Landmark and Historic District Building Database gpmc-yuvp (37c)
fruit.notes: The architectural record beneath each designation — architect, up to three styles, up to five materials, original use, construction and alteration dates, per BBL/BIN.

fruit.name: Historic Districts
fruit.notes: Area designations covering many buildings; extensions flagged; LP_NUMBER key, polygon area/length, designation and hearing dates. The map is LPC's most-viewed Open Data asset.

fruit.name: LPC Permit History (issued)
fruit.notes: Open READ twin of processed permits — docket, regulation type (Certificate of Appropriateness / No Effect), work types, applicant/owner, received/issue/expiration dates. The read side of what Portico writes.

fruit.name: Designation & Archaeology Reports
fruit.notes: The documents that establish significance. Designation report PDFs are linked from buis-pvji REPORT_URL and the site index; archaeology reports are their own dataset.

fruit.name: Landmark Complaints & Violations
fruit.notes: Enforcement records — complaints of unpermitted work, warning letters, Notices of Violation, and rescissions; keyed on LPC# / Complaint # and BBL/BIN.

fruit.name: Grant Applications
fruit.notes: Historic-preservation grant applications and their board/staff actions — a secondary open tracking dataset alongside the permit record.

fruit.name: Search permit history (Permit Application Finder)
fruit.notes: The public Permit Application Finder / map is a UI over the same dpm2-m9mq record; read is covered, the search itself is not an owned API.

fruit.name: Explore the Discover NYC Landmarks map
fruit.notes: Esri ArcGIS Online web map. Its hosted feature services are queryable via ArcGIS REST but are not an owned or documented LPC API; a second read silo alongside Socrata.

fruit.name: File a permit application (Certificate of Appropriateness / No Effect)
fruit.notes: The core owner/architect transaction — request an LPC permit. No API and no write twin; lives only inside the Salesforce Portico community (or on paper). The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.
